title: Voyager Free 60+ — Primary Earbud State Machine: In-Ear Connected → In-Case Lid-Closed Disconnected (Left Primary)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in ears. App showing Device screen.
Note the current primary earbud (left by default). Verify Device screen shows both earbuds as 'Connected' with no charging icon.
Remove LEFT earbud (primary) first and place it into the charge case. Do NOT close lid yet.
Observe app Device screen: verify LEFT earbud shows 'In Case' or 'Charging' icon and transitions to 'Disconnected' state within 5 seconds.
Verify RIGHT earbud (secondary) remains 'Connected' and takes over as primary (audio routing switches).
Now place RIGHT earbud into the charge case. Do NOT close lid yet.
Observe app Device screen: verify RIGHT earbud shows 'In Case' or 'Charging' icon and transitions to 'Disconnected' state within 5 seconds.
Verify both earbuds now show 'Disconnected' / 'In Case' state.
Close the charge case lid.
Verify app Device screen shows both earbuds as 'Disconnected' with charging indicators. Verify neither earbud shows a 'Connected' badge.
expected: Expected: LEFT earbud (primary) transitions from 'Connected' → 'In Case/Charging' → 'Disconnected' within 5s of being placed in case. RIGHT earbud takes over as primary. After both in case + lid closed, both show 'Disconnected' with charging icon. Actual (If Bug): LEFT earbud remains 'Connected' with a charging icon overlay after lid close — incorrect dual-state (Connected + Charging). Regression boundary: Primary earbud must fully disconnect; secondary must assume primary role.

title: Voyager Free 60+ — Primary Earbud State Machine: In-Ear Connected → In-Case Lid-Closed Disconnected (Right Primary via Role Swap)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in ears.
Force primary role to RIGHT earbud: remove LEFT earbud from ear, wait for RIGHT to become primary, then reinsert LEFT.
Verify app Device screen shows RIGHT earbud as primary (audio routing indicator on right).
Remove RIGHT earbud (primary) first and place it into the charge case. Do NOT close lid.
Observe app Device screen: verify RIGHT earbud shows 'In Case' or 'Charging' icon and transitions to 'Disconnected' within 5 seconds.
Verify LEFT earbud (now secondary) remains 'Connected' and takes over as primary.
Place LEFT earbud into charge case. Do NOT close lid.
Verify LEFT earbud shows 'In Case' / 'Charging' and transitions to 'Disconnected' within 5 seconds.
Close the charge case lid.
Verify app Device screen shows both earbuds as 'Disconnected' with charging indicators. Verify neither shows 'Connected'.
expected: Expected: RIGHT earbud (primary) transitions 'Connected' → 'In Case/Charging' → 'Disconnected' within 5s. LEFT takes over as primary. After both in case + lid closed, both show 'Disconnected'. Actual (If Bug): RIGHT earbud remains 'Connected' with charging icon — same asymmetric failure as left-primary bug but on the right side. Regression boundary: Role-swapped primary must also fully disconnect on case insertion + lid close.

title: Voyager Free 60+ — Lid-Close Bluetooth Disconnect Signal Validation (Case-to-Phone)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in charge case, lid OPEN. App shows both earbuds as 'In Case' / 'Charging' and 'Disconnected'.
Remove both earbuds from case, insert in ears. Close case lid (empty case).
Verify app shows both earbuds as 'Connected' within 5 seconds.
Remove both earbuds from ears and place them into the charge case. Leave lid OPEN.
Verify app shows both earbuds transition to 'In Case' / 'Charging' and 'Disconnected' within 5 seconds.
Close the charge case lid.
Verify app Device screen shows both earbuds as 'Disconnected' with charging indicators. Verify Bluetooth icon on phone shows no active BT connection to the earbuds (or shows 'Not Connected').
Open the charge case lid.
Verify app does NOT automatically reconnect earbuds (they should remain disconnected until physically removed from case).
Remove LEFT earbud from case. Verify it reconnects and shows 'Connected' in app.
expected: Expected: Closing the lid with earbuds inside triggers a clean BT disconnect signal from case to phone. App shows 'Disconnected' + charging icon. Opening lid does NOT auto-reconnect — earbuds stay disconnected until removed from case. Actual (If Bug): App continues showing 'Connected' after lid close, or shows stale 'Connected' state with charging icon overlay — indicating the case failed to send (or phone failed to process) the lid-close disconnect signal. Regression boundary: Lid-close must trigger definitive disconnect; lid-open must not auto-reconnect.

title: Voyager Free 60+ — Immediate State Trigger: Place Primary Earbud in Case + Close Lid, Kill App, Reopen
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in ears. App in foreground on Device screen.
Remove LEFT earbud (primary) and place it into the charge case. IMMEDIATELY close the lid (within 2 seconds). Do NOT wait for app UI to update.
IMMEDIATELY force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents).
Wait 10 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device screen.
Observe the state of both earbuds.
Open the charge case lid. Remove LEFT earbud. Observe reconnection behavior.
expected: Expected: After app reopen, both earbuds show 'Disconnected' with charging indicators. LEFT earbud does NOT show 'Connected' state. When LEFT earbud is removed from case, it reconnects normally. Actual (If Bug): LEFT earbud shows 'Connected' with charging icon overlay — the app cached a stale 'Connected' state before the lid-close disconnect signal was processed, and the force-kill prevented the disconnect callback from being handled. Regression boundary: App must reconcile earbud state on relaunch by querying current BT connection status, not relying on pre-kill cached state.

title: Voyager Free 60+ — Deep Lifecycle: Lid Close → BT Disconnect → Phone Reboot → App Relaunch
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in ears.
Remove both earbuds and place into charge case. Close lid.
Verify app shows both earbuds as 'Disconnected' with charging indicators.
Reboot the phone.
After phone reboots, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device screen.
Verify both earbuds show as 'Disconnected' (no stale 'Connected' state).
Open charge case lid. Remove LEFT earbud. Verify it connects and shows 'Connected' in app.
Remove RIGHT earbud. Verify both show 'Connected'.
expected: Expected: After phone reboot + app relaunch, both earbuds show 'Disconnected' with no stale connection state. Earbuds connect normally when removed from case. Actual (If Bug): App shows one or both earbuds as 'Connected' after reboot — indicates the app or BT stack cached a connection state that survived reboot, or the case's lid-close disconnect was not properly persisted. Regression boundary: Phone reboot must clear any stale BT connection cache; app must show true disconnected state.

title: Voyager Free 60+ — Touchscreen Case Interaction: Lid Close While App is on Transparency Mode Settings Screen
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in ears.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode.
Set Environment Surround level to 75. Set Speech Free level to 60.
Switch to Speech sub-setting tab. Verify Speech shows 60.
Remove both earbuds and place into charge case. Close lid while remaining on the Transparency Mode screen.
Observe app UI: does it show any error, loading state, or stale connection indicators?
Wait 10 seconds. Navigate back to Device screen.
Verify both earbuds show 'Disconnected' with charging indicators.
Open case lid. Remove LEFT earbud. Wait for connection.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode.
Switch to Environment sub-setting. Verify Surround level is still 75.
Switch to Speech sub-setting. Verify Free level is still 60.
expected: Expected: Closing lid while on Transparency Mode screen does not crash the app or show stale 'Connected' state. After lid close, navigating to Device screen shows both earbuds as 'Disconnected'. After reconnect, Transparency Mode values persist (Surround=75, Speech=60). Actual (If Bug): App shows earbuds as 'Connected' on Device screen even after lid close, or Transparency Mode values reset to defaults after the disconnect/reconnect cycle. Regression boundary: Lid-close disconnect must not corrupt in-flight UI state or pending settings persistence.

title: Voyager Free 60+ — Asymmetric Disconnect: Remove Secondary First, Then Primary, Close Lid
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 60+ connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Both earbuds in ears.
Remove RIGHT earbud (secondary) first and place into charge case. Do NOT close lid.
Verify app shows RIGHT as 'In Case' / 'Charging' / 'Disconnected'. Verify LEFT (primary) remains 'Connected'.
Remove LEFT earbud (primary) and place into charge case. Do NOT close lid.
Verify app shows LEFT as 'In Case' / 'Charging' / 'Disconnected' within 5 seconds.
Verify both earbuds show 'Disconnected' state.
Close the charge case lid.
Verify app Device screen shows both earbuds as 'Disconnected' with charging indicators. Verify no 'Connected' badge on either earbud.
Open lid. Remove LEFT earbud. Verify it reconnects as primary.
Remove RIGHT earbud. Verify both connected.
expected: Expected: Both earbuds transition to 'Disconnected' regardless of removal order. Secondary removal first does not interfere with primary's subsequent disconnect. After lid close, both show 'Disconnected' + charging. Actual (If Bug): LEFT (primary) remains 'Connected' after being placed in case and lid closed — the bug manifests specifically when primary is placed in case after secondary, suggesting a race condition in the primary role handoff. Regression boundary: Removal order must not affect primary earbud's ability to disconnect when placed in case with lid closed.
